topic: Gemini CLI Guide 2026: Install, Setup, Features, and Workflows

competitors.title: Gemini CLI vs Claude Code (2026 Comparison): Which Terminal AI Coding Agent Wins?
key_points: Gemini CLI is fully open-source (Apache 2.0) with 1M+ token context window, multimodal inputs (images, PDFs, diagrams), MCP integration, and Google Search grounding
Free tier: thousands of daily requests with Google account — no credit card required; API pay-as-you-go is very competitive, especially with efficient Flash models
Gemini CLI dominates in speed/throughput — lower latency ideal for rapid iteration, log parsing, infrastructure scripting, throwaway prototypes; handles massive contexts without fragmentation
Claude Code focuses on thoughtful reasoning, multi-file autonomous workflows, built-in safety layers with visual diffs and rollback previews
Teams using Claude Code see 2–5× faster feature completion on involved tasks; Gemini CLI users rave about velocity for experimentation and high-volume simple operations
Hybrid workflow common in 2026: start with Gemini CLI for idea exploration/boilerplate, switch to Claude Code for thoughtful implementation/review
Alternatives: Cursor, GitHub Copilot (Workspace/Agent), Aider, OpenAI Codex CLI, Goose/Cline
2026–2027 trends: multi-agent orchestration, tighter IDE fusion, voice/natural-language control, agents handling entire features autonomously

competitors.title: Gemini CLI: your open-source AI agent (Google Official Announcement)
key_points: Gemini CLI is an open-source AI agent bringing Gemini directly into terminal — lightweight, most direct path from prompt to model
Unmatched free usage: 60 model requests per minute, 1,000 requests per day at no charge — industry's largest allowance
Free tier gives access to Gemini 2.5 Pro with massive 1 million token context window
Built-in tools: Google Search grounding, MCP support, customizable prompts/instructions (GEMINI.md), non-interactive scripting mode
Integrated with Gemini Code Assist — same technology in VS Code and terminal; agent mode available free on all plans
Open source (Apache 2.0) — inspect code, contribute, community-driven development
Versatile beyond coding: content generation, problem solving, deep research, task management
Easy start: install, login with Google account (email address only), begin using — under a minute setup

competitors.title: How to Install Gemini CLI: Complete Setup Guide for All Platforms (2026)
key_points: Latest stable version: v0.32.1 (March 2026), powered by Gemini 3 with 1M token context; Gemini 3.1 Pro Preview available since v0.31.0
96,600+ GitHub stars as of March 2026 — one of most popular developer tools in AI ecosystem
7 installation methods: npm global install (recommended), Homebrew, npx (no-install trial), Docker, and platform-specific package managers
3 authentication options: Google OAuth (free — 60 RPM / 1,000 RPD), Google AI Studio API key (pay-as-you-go), Vertex AI key (enterprise billing)
System requirements: Node.js 20+ (22 LTS recommended), macOS 15+, Windows 11 24H2+, Ubuntu 20.04+, 4GB RAM minimum (16GB recommended)
Built-in safety model: explicit user approval for every file modification and shell command; sandbox mode via Docker/Podman; checkpointing system for rollback
GEMINI.md files for project-level customization (like CLAUDE.md), MCP server integration, configurable settings.json
Shell compatibility: Bash, Zsh, PowerShell out of box; Fish has limited support; Windows Terminal + PowerShell or WSL2 recommended
